name: 12-dpop-multi-client-sso
hypothesis: Two DPoP-bound OIDC clients in one realm share one browser SSO session: after an interactive login to client A, client B authorizes with NO second login (near-instant SSO) yet still mints an access token DPoP-bound to client B's OWN key.
_provides: two DPoP-required public OIDC clients 'dpop-app-a' & 'dpop-app-b' (auth-code flow + PKCE, redirect to the test-app DPoP harness http://localhost:3000/dpop-harness, attribute dpop.bound.access.tokens=true) + a user 'ssouser' that is Tide-linked out-of-band (see _tideSetup) so the spec's interactive enclave login can succeed.
_enclaveGap: CLOSED. The probe uses the non-DPoP admin-cli client (a DPoP-required client would reject a password grant that carries no DPoP proof), so it only baselines that the realm ORK-signs — it stays on the API path and never forces the Heimdall browser enclave (per the iga-engine hard rules). The recipe itself still only creates a PLAIN user; the spec's interactive browser login is satisfied because provisionScenario() Tide-links 'ssouser' out-of-band via tide-admin-cli (the _tideSetup overlay below), so the realm's enclave widget accepts it. The DPoP token binding (cnf.jkt) and the SSO session cookie remain runtime/browser concerns exercised by the spec, not recipe setup.
